journalEntries.content: I cancelled the plans I had made for today. It seemed easier to stay home than to face the effort of going out.
[PERSON_a1a1a1a1-a1a1-a1a1-a1a1-a1a1a1a1a1a1] sent word asking how I was. I did not reply. There is nothing worth saying.
I intended to go for a walk this morning. By mid-morning the intention had dissolved entirely.
Everything feels distant and difficult to reach. I cannot find the motivation to begin even small tasks.
I have been putting off writing letters for two weeks now. It never seems like the right time.
[PERSON_a1a1a1a1-a1a1-a1a1-a1a1-a1a1a1a1a1a1] visited briefly. Afterwards I felt more exhausted than before the visit.
The day passed without me doing anything of note. This has become the pattern.
I know I should be active. The knowledge does not help. I remain where I am.
I looked at the list of activities I had planned and put it away in a drawer. Tomorrow, perhaps.
[PERSON_a1a1a1a1-a1a1-a1a1-a1a1-a1a1a1a1a1a1] seems to manage everything with ease. I cannot understand how.
I attempted breakfast and left half of it. Appetite has been absent for days.
The simplest decisions feel enormous. Choosing which chair to sit in requires more energy than I have.
I fell asleep in the afternoon again. When I woke the day was gone and nothing had been done.
I have not left the house in four days. Each morning I plan to, and each evening I have not.
[PERSON_a1a1a1a1-a1a1-a1a1-a1a1-a1a1a1a1a1a1] wrote again. I folded the letter and left it on the table where I cannot avoid seeing it.
I used to find meaning in small routines. Now the routines feel like obligations I resent.
I sat by the window for an hour. I am not sure what I was waiting for.
Someone asked if I was well. I said yes. The answer felt dishonest but the truth felt impossible to explain.
I started to read and could not concentrate. The words were there but carried no meaning.
[PERSON_a1a1a1a1-a1a1-a1a1-a1a1-a1a1a1a1a1a1] says action is the cure for inertia. I cannot find the beginning of an action.
The day has ended. I achieved nothing I intended to. Tomorrow will likely be the same.
I managed to dress and eat. These feel like significant achievements, which tells me how far I have fallen.
The effort required to respond to correspondence seems disproportionate to the task itself.
I went as far as the front door and turned back. This is not the person I want to be.
[PERSON_a1a1a1a1-a1a1-a1a1-a1a1-a1a1a1a1a1a1] is never still. I used to admire it. Now it simply exhausts me to observe.
Nothing feels worth the effort of beginning. Everything is too far away.
I opened a window. This is the most purposeful thing I have done today.
I keep a list of things I mean to do. The list grows. The doing does not.
[PERSON_a1a1a1a1-a1a1-a1a1-a1a1-a1a1a1a1a1a1] noticed I had not been myself. I could not tell him when it started.
The weight of inaction is heavier than any action I might take. Yet I remain inactive.

activityHierarchy.taskName: Walk to the end of the street and back
Prepare and eat a full meal
Meet a friend for a short visit
Return to professional practice for a morning
